_meta.authored_from: §3.6 (kid header, multi-key verifier pattern) + §3.3 (alg: dir, enc: A256GCM, HKDF derivation), cross-service-contract 2026-05-25
_meta.author: ChrisPachulski
notes: Tests the §3.6 verifier dispatch pattern: decodeProtectedHeader(token).kid → keyMap.get(kid) → jwtDecrypt.
Key derivation: HKDF-Extract+Expand (RFC 5869, SHA-256) over the raw secret with salt='' and info='eex/device-token/v<N>'.
v1 test secret (32 bytes UTF-8): DEVICE_TEST_SECRET_V1_32CHARS_XX
v2 test secret (32 bytes UTF-8): DEVICE_TEST_SECRET_V2_32CHARS_XX
alg: dir means the encrypted key component (part[1] of the compact serialization) is empty.
JWE tokens are generated with jose CompactEncrypt; they are NOT deterministic (IV is random). Consumers MUST decrypt and compare the claims object, not the token bytes.
ULID charset check: 01HWXYZ01234567890ABCDEFGS, 01HWXYZ01234567890ABCDEFGT, 01HWXYZ01234567890ABCDEFGV, 01HWXYZ01234567890ABCDEFGW — all Crockford Base32, no I/L/O/U.
These vectors are the ground truth for TS (D-row deviceTokenMiddleware), Rust (M2+ emerald-contracts verifier), Swift (M2 EmeraldKit decryptDeviceToken).

vectors.name: device-v1-known-kid-passes
vectors.description: Token with kid='device-v1' and the correct v1 key decrypts successfully. Tests the happy path of the §3.6 kid-dispatch verifier: decodeProtectedHeader returns kid='device-v1', keyMap.get('device-v1') returns the derived v1 key, jwtDecrypt succeeds.
vectors.decryptionNote: Decrypt with keyMap['device-v1'].derivedKeyHex. Assert decrypted claims match expectedClaims. Token bytes are NOT stable (random IV); do not compare token bytes.
vectors.sampleTokenNote: sampleToken is a pre-generated example encrypted with derivedKeyHex for device-v1. Test harnesses SHOULD generate a fresh token from expectedClaims + kid + derivedKeyHex and verify round-trip. The sampleToken is provided as a cross-language sanity fixture only.

vectors.name: device-v2-known-kid-passes
vectors.description: Token with kid='device-v2' and the correct v2 key decrypts successfully. Tests that the verifier correctly dispatches to the v2 key during a rotation overlap window. Both v1 and v2 tokens must be accepted simultaneously.
vectors.decryptionNote: Decrypt with keyMap['device-v2'].derivedKeyHex. Assert decrypted claims match expectedClaims.
vectors.sampleTokenNote: sampleToken is a pre-generated example encrypted with derivedKeyHex for device-v2. Same note as device-v1-known-kid-passes.

vectors.name: unknown-kid-hard-rejects
vectors.description: Token with kid='device-v99' (not in keyMap) must be hard-rejected before decryption is attempted. Tests the §3.6 guard: if keyMap.get(kid) returns undefined, throw immediately with a clear error. Do NOT attempt to iterate all keys — that enables kid enumeration attacks.
vectors.errorNote: The verifier MUST throw/return an error containing 'unknown kid' or equivalent before any decryption attempt. The token bytes need not be a valid JWE — the kid check fires first on the result of decodeProtectedHeader().
vectors.syntheticTokenNote: This is a structurally plausible JWE compact with kid='device-v99' in the protected header. The ciphertext/tag are dummy bytes — decryption would fail even if attempted. The test asserts the verifier rejects on kid lookup, not on decryption.

vectors.name: missing-kid-hard-rejects
vectors.description: Token with no kid field in the protected header must be hard-rejected. Tests the null/undefined guard in the verifier: kid = decodeProtectedHeader(token).kid; if !kid or keyMap.get(kid ?? '') returns undefined, throw immediately. A token without kid cannot be dispatched to any key.
vectors.errorNote: Same rejection path as unknown-kid-hard-rejects. The verifier coalesces absent kid to '' (per the §3.6 reference implementation: keyMap.get(kid ?? '')), which is also not in keyMap.
vectors.syntheticTokenNote: Protected header has alg+enc but no kid field. decodeProtectedHeader().kid is undefined. The verifier must catch this.
